cluster.title: Xiaomi Unveils 17 Ultra Smartphone with Leica Camera and Powerbank Accessory

synthesis.narrative:
## Xiaomi Launches 17 Series Smartphones with Leica Partnership

Xiaomi unveiled its flagship 17 series smartphones on February 28, 2026, ahead of Mobile World Congress in Barcelona, featuring an expanded partnership with German camera manufacturer Leica [1][3][4].

### Key Device Lineup

The launch includes three main variants: the standard Xiaomi 17, the premium 17 Ultra, and a special Leica-branded Leitzphone edition of the Ultra [3][4]. Notably absent from the global launch is the 17 Pro, which launched in China with an additional rear display but has not been announced for international markets [3].

### Camera Technology and Leica Partnership

The partnership with Leica represents a significant milestone, as this marks the first time Leica has allowed Xiaomi to use its red dot logo on hardware, despite their collaboration since 2022 [4]. The 17 Ultra features a sophisticated camera system with a 50-megapixel main sensor using a 1-inch sensor and F/1.67 aperture [1]. The standout feature is a 200-megapixel telephoto camera with variable focal length equivalent to 75mm-100mm, providing optical zoom between 3.2x and 4.3x [1][3].

### Battery Performance and Charging

Battery capacity varies across models and regions. The 17 Ultra includes a 6,000mAh battery globally, though the Chinese version features a larger 6,800mAh capacity [1][2]. The standard Xiaomi 17 has a 6,330mAh battery [1][3]. All models support fast charging, with the Ultra supporting 90W wired charging and 50W wireless charging [1][2].

### Pricing and Availability

The devices will be available in the UK, Europe, and select other markets, but not officially in the US [2][3]. Pricing starts at £899/€999 for the Xiaomi 17, £1,299/€1,499 for the 17 Ultra, and £1,699/€1,999 for the Leitzphone edition [3][4]. Xiaomi also announced photography accessories, including the 17 Ultra Photography Kit (€99.99) and Photography Kit Pro (€199.99) [1].

### Software and Features

All devices run Xiaomi's HyperOS 3 based on Android 16, with the Leitzphone featuring customized Leica-themed interface elements including monochrome app icons and dedicated photography widgets [4]. The phones are powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor [1][3].

citations.title: Xiaomi launches 17 Ultra smartphone, an AirTag clone, and an ultra slim powerbank
citations.quote: The phone has a 50-megapixel main sensor with an F/1.67 aperture and a 1-inch sensor. But the main attraction is the 200-megapixel telephoto camera that has a variable focal length of 75mm-100mm equivalent.

citations.title: Review: Xiaomi 17 Ultra and Leitzphone Pack Leica Magic Into a Flagship Phone
citations.quote: Xiaomi has managed to pack a 6,000-mAh battery into the 17 Ultra (up from 5,410 mAh last year). The jump gives it serious stamina, and this phone can go a couple of days between charges.

citations.title: Xiaomi 17 is a small(ish) phone with a big(ish) battery
citations.quote: The 17 and 17 Ultra will apparently be available soon in the UK, Europe, and select other markets. The 17 — pitched as a rival to the likes of the iPhone 17 and Samsung Galaxy S26 — will cost £899 / €999

citations.title: Xiaomi's Leica Leitzphone mostly earns the name
citations.quote: Leica hasn't let Xiaomi use its red dot logo on hardware until now, even though the companies have partnered on Xiaomi's flagship cameras since 2022. The jump to a fully Leica-branded phone is a vote of confidence from the camera company.

deepAnalysis.historicalContext: This launch represents the culmination of Xiaomi's multi-year strategy to move upmarket from its budget-focused origins. The Leica partnership, which began in 2022, has now reached unprecedented depth with the first-ever use of Leica's iconic red dot logo on Xiaomi hardware. This mirrors similar premium partnerships in the industry, such as Huawei's collaboration with Leica (which ended due to US sanctions) and OnePlus's partnership with Hasselblad. Xiaomi's exclusion from the US market continues a pattern established during the Trump administration's trade tensions, while the company has successfully expanded in Europe and other international markets. The variable telephoto camera technology represents a significant technical advancement in smartphone photography, building on years of computational photography improvements across the industry.
implications: Intensified competition in premium smartphone camera technology, potentially accelerating innovation cycles across the industry
Strengthened position for Chinese smartphone manufacturers in European markets, challenging traditional Samsung and Apple dominance
Validation of camera-focused partnerships as a key differentiator in saturated smartphone markets
Continued fragmentation of global smartphone availability due to geopolitical tensions, particularly affecting US consumers
Potential pricing pressure on established premium brands as Xiaomi offers flagship features at competitive price points
Growing importance of photography accessories and ecosystem products as revenue diversification for smartphone manufacturers
Demonstration that premium camera branding can successfully transition from traditional photography to smartphone markets
